patient.name: Bryce Ross
patient.medical_history: No significant medical history, plays recreational basketball

timeline.summary: Triaged as NON_URGENT. Patient experiencing mild knee discomfort and occasional swelling. Sent to doctor for evaluation.

details.triage_reasoning: Patient presents with mild knee discomfort and occasional swelling, consistent with a minor injury. Current symptoms indicate stability with no serious underlying conditions.
details.notes: Patient has no significant medical history and is not taking any current medications. Symptoms do not require immediate intervention and can be managed outpatient.
details.context_for_next: The doctor will assess further and provide a treatment plan for the knee discomfort.
details.viewer_output: Triaged as NON_URGENT. Patient experiencing mild knee discomfort and occasional swelling. Sent to doctor for evaluation.

timeline.summary: Diagnosis: Mild knee sprain. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: R.I.C.E. and NSAIDs for pain relief.

details.recommended_treatment: R.I.C.E. (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation), along with NSAIDs for pain relief as needed. Consider physiotherapy if symptoms persist.
details.reasoning: The patient's symptoms of mild discomfort and occasional swelling align with a mild knee sprain based on similar cases and relevant experiences. No tests have been completed, but previous cases suggest conservative management is appropriate. High-risk features were not present, and the patient has stable vitals. Treatment plan involves rest and symptom management.
details.learning_principles_used: Used case #1: Provided support for diagnosing knee issues with mild symptoms. Applied principle: R.I.C.E. method is a standard approach.
details.viewer_output: Diagnosis: Mild knee sprain. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: R.I.C.E. and NSAIDs for pain relief.

timeline.summary: Learned: Knee discomfort with occasional swelling and difficulty squatting requires MRI to assess for meniscus tear. This will improve future diagnoses of knee injuries.

details.principle_text: Knee discomfort with occasional swelling and difficulty squatting requires MRI to assess for meniscus tear.
details.reasoning: Failure to order appropriate imaging led to misdiagnosis of a meniscus tear as a mild sprain, missing the correct treatment path.
details.viewer_output: Learned: Knee discomfort with occasional swelling and difficulty squatting requires MRI to assess for meniscus tear. This will improve future diagnoses of knee injuries.
